prompt_md: A team is wiring an AI coding agent into their development pipeline. The agent can edit files, run shell commands, and open pull requests. **Select every option that is a legitimate guardrail control** (acting on the agent's input, its actions, or its output).

choices: Validating and sanitizing issue/PR text before the agent treats it as a task
Running the agent in a sandbox with scoped file, command, and network permissions
Maintaining an allow/deny list so safe commands run but 'rm -rf' and force-push are blocked
Scanning the agent's diff for secrets or PII before it can commit
Choosing a model with a larger context window so it can read more of the codebase
Requiring a human review/approval gate before an AI-generated change merges or deploys
